signature_events.intro: Get caught up in the excitement of live entertainment, farm-fresh and delicious food, and holiday cheer with Downtown Chico's signature events! These community-strengthening events are a huge part of what makes Chico so special, and have been recognized as some of the best across the North State.

restaurants.intro: Downtown Chico is buzzing with great restaurants to discover from morning to night. You'll find classic hot spots to a whole new restaurant scene that's putting Downtown Chico on the culinary map. With more street side dining and outdoor patios than ever before, come enjoy outdoor dining and people watching in the heart of Downtown Chico.

 Attendees can stroll through downtown from 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM, enjoying flavorful offerings and live performances from local musicians along the way. The day culminates with a free, family-friendly concert at City Plaza from 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM featuring the acclaimed band Monkey Wrench .

items.content:
ARTober FEST

 ARTober FEST 2025 Event Guide

 Overview

 The ARTober FEST celebration slated for October seeks to merge artistic expression with community engagement, creating a dynamic gathering that celebrates the rich cultural tapestry of Chico. Through partnerships with the Museum of Northern California Art (MONCA), Chico Art Center, Chico Arts Commission, Chico Arts & Culture Foundation, Chico Visual Arts Alliance, local educational institutions such as Butte College and Chico State, and a diverse array of local artists and organizations, ARTober FEST features a wide range of activities including live performances, art exhibitions, and workshops.

 Signature Events

- October 10 – Art & Wine Walk (5:00 – 8:00 PM): Enjoy the Downtown Chico Business Association Annual Art & Wine Walk! Since 2015 this event has showcased local artists, regional wine, beer, and cider tastings, and live music throughout Downtown Chico. Purchase your tickets today for only $20!

- October 11 – Harvest Sidewalk Sale (10:00 AM – 5:00 PM): Rake in the savings and celebrate the changing of the season! The Harvest Sidewalk Sale is the perfect opportunity to pick up some incredible deals and get a jump-start on holiday shopping.

- October 30 – Treat Street (2:00 – 5:00 PM): Join us for a fun evening of trick-or-treating, live music and photo opportunities with Chico PD and Fire!

 All Month Long

- October 1–31 – ARTober FEST Month Long Art Walk: Stroll through Downtown Chico to view the work of local artists in participating downtown businesses!

- October 1–31 (Fridays & Saturdays) – Chico Haunted Tours: Born from a group of friends with a love for ghost stories and local lore, Chico Haunted Tours takes you deep into the city's spine-tingling past. Every step is steeped in thoroughly researched history, and every ticket helps restore and preserve the legendary El Rey Theatre - a landmark as beautiful as it is haunted. Visit ChicoHauntedTours.com for more info.

- October 1–31 – Nexo Entre Raices at Janet Turner Print Museum: The Turner is excited to host Nexo Entre Raices, a portfolio bringing together 33 contemporary artists living and working on either side of the US-Mexico border. Organized by Marco Sanchez at Taller La Espina, this compelling print portfolio explores how our cultural roots shape and define us. Don't miss the Artist Talk & Opening Reception on Thursday, Oct. 2 from 5:30–7:30 PM in the Performing Arts Center (PAC) 134 at CSU Chico.

items.title: Small Business Saturday
items.content:
Small Business Saturday

 Date: Saturday, November 29, 2025

 Time: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM

 Location: Downtown Chico

 Overview

 The holiday season is here, and there's no better time to shop small and support the businesses that make Downtown Chico unique. From boutiques and gift shops to restaurants and specialty services, our downtown is full of one-of-a-kind experiences you won't find anywhere else.

 When you choose to spend locally, you're doing more than making a purchase—you're strengthening our community. Did you know that $68 of every $100 spent at a small business stays right here in the local economy, supporting local jobs, nonprofits, suppliers, and essential services?

 Join us on Saturday, November 29, 2025, from 10:00 AM–5:00 PM as Downtown Chico businesses celebrate Small Business Saturday with special sales, promotions, and holiday cheer. It's the perfect day to shop, explore, and make a meaningful impact in the place we all call home.

 Receipt-to-Win Raffle

 And to make it even sweeter…

 To encourage shopping and dining downtown, the DCBA , in partnership with ChicoBag , is offering complimentary Candy Cane Shopping Bags to the first 100 shoppers who bring a receipt of $30 or more to the DCBA office (338 Broadway) during Small Business Saturday . Limit one per customer. Available while supplies last.

Show a receipt or proof of purchase of $30 or more from any Downtown Chico business on Small Business Saturday to receive a raffle ticket for our Shop Small Raffle.

338 Broadway Street, Chico

 Prizes include:

• Curated gift baskets

 This holiday season: shop small, support local.

items.title: Slice of Chico
items.content:
Thank You!

We are officially getting close to the 2026 Slice of Chico event! Thank you to everyone who joined us in the past to celebrate summer, local shopping, and ice-cold watermelon! Stay tuned for updates on this year's Slice of Chico festivities in Downtown Chico.

 Slice of Chico

 Date: Saturday, July 11th, 2026

 Time: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM

 Location: Downtown Chico

 Overview

 Now celebrating its 51st year, Slice of Chico brings the community together with complimentary ice-cold watermelon slices and a showcase of Chico's diverse local shops and restaurants. This summertime tradition transforms Downtown Chico into a lively marketplace filled with food, shopping, entertainment, and fun for all ages.

 The watermelon slicing stations will open at 10:00 AM, with the main station hosted by the Downtown Chico Business Association (DCBA) on W. 3rd Street between Broadway and Main Street.

 Visitors can enjoy sidewalks filled with vibrant activity, featuring special sales from boutiques, unique finds from local artisans, and delicious offerings from Chico's many restaurants.

 Street closures in the downtown area will begin at 9:00 AM and reopen at 6:00 PM. This includes W. 3rd Street between Broadway and Main Street.

items.content:
Event Update: The 2025 Art & Wine Walk has concluded. Thank you to everyone who joined us in Downtown Chico for an evening of art, wine, and community! Stay tuned for details on next year's event, we can't wait to welcome you back.

 Art & Wine Walk

 Get Tickets Here

-->
 Date: Friday, October 9, 2025

 Time: 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM

 Location: Downtown Chico

 Overview

 The Art & Wine Walk is Downtown Chico's evening celebration of local art and culture. Attendees stroll through downtown, explore gallery receptions and in-store exhibits, and enjoy curated tastings of wine, beer, and cider. Live music and special installations create a welcoming atmosphere throughout the district.

 What to Expect

- Tastings: Sample wine, beer, and cider at participating downtown businesses.

- Art Showcases: View works by local artists presented in galleries, storefronts, and pop-up spaces.

- Live Music: Musicians and small ensembles will be scheduled at select locations to enhance the experience.

 View Full List of Participants Here

 Tickets

 Get Tickets Here

-->
 Ticket sales for the 2025 event have ended. Please check back next year for new ticket information and event updates.

 Event Policies

- Age & ID: You must be 21+ to consume alcohol. Valid government-issued photo ID is required at check in and may be re-checked at tasting locations.

- Tasting Glasses & Wristband Required for Tasting: To participate in alcohol tasting, you must purchase a ticket and check in at one of our designated Ticket Stations on the day of the event. Upon checking in, you will receive a commemorative tasting glass and a wristband. You will not be served alcohol without these items.

- Responsible Service: Tasting sizes are limited in accordance with ABC licensing (wine/beer = 3oz pour max, spirits = 1oz pour max). Please limit tastings to one per person, per each participating business. All alcohol must be consumed prior to leaving downtown business (discard receptacles will be available to dispose of unwanted tastings).

- Accessibility: Downtown sidewalks and crossings are generally accessible. If you need accommodation, please contact us in advance so we can assist.

- Weather: The event is planned to proceed rain or shine.

items.title: Thursday Night Market
items.subtitle: Every Thursday from 6:00-9:00PM
items.content:
Vendor applications for the 2026 Thursday Night Market season are now available!

 Thursday Night Market 2026

 Season Dates: April 9 – September 24, 2026

 Time: Thursdays | 6:00 PM – 9:00 PM

 Location: Downtown Chico - on Main & Broadway

 See Event Maps

 View Downtown Businesses at the Market

 The Thursday Night Market transforms Downtown Chico into a vibrant, open-air celebration of community each week from April through September. With over 200 vendors, the market brings together local farmers, makers, food vendors, nonprofits, and small businesses for an experience that is as dynamic as it is welcoming.

 Stroll through downtown and discover fresh, locally grown produce, handmade goods, and unique finds while enjoying a lively lineup of musicians, performers, and entertainers. Each month introduces a new themed experience, from health and wellness to outdoor adventure, cultural celebration, nonprofit engagement, weddings, and even pets, giving every visit something new to explore.

 Families can enjoy expanded kids' activities and interactive experiences, while community organizations and local services connect with residents in meaningful ways. With bike valet available, late-night shopping and dining, and a continually evolving mix of vendors, the market offers something for everyone.

 More than just an event, the Thursday Night Market is a weekly tradition, an opportunity to gather, support local, and celebrate what makes Chico feel like home.

 The market takes place rain or shine (cancelled only if temperatures exceed 110° during market hours). Per CA Health & Safety Code 114259.5, only service animals are permitted.

 Vendor Information

 Vendor Information

 For vendor questions, contact us at dcba@downtownchico.com or call (530) 345-6500.

 Office hours: Tuesday–Friday, 10 AM – 5 PM at 338 Broadway St, Chico, CA.

 Facebook Event Link

 Market Highlights

- 200+ vendors featuring local food, produce, handmade goods, and more

- Monthly themed nights and community expos

- Live music, performers, and entertainment each week

- Expanded kids' activities and family-friendly experiences

- Community organizations and local resources

- Bike valet available on-site

- Downtown shops and restaurants open late

- Weekly updated market map with vendors and amenities

- Rain or shine (cancelled only if temps exceed 110° during market hours)

- Per CA Health & Safety Code 114259.5, only service animals permitted

 Award: Voted 2024 C&R's Best Family-Friendly Event

 NEW: Themed Expos at Thursday Night Market

 This year, the Thursday Night Market is evolving in an exciting new way.

 We're introducing monthly themed expos—dynamic, curated experiences designed to highlight different industries, celebrate community, and bring fresh energy to Downtown Chico throughout the season.

 Hosted in Chico City Plaza, each month will feature a unique theme, creating interactive spaces where visitors can discover, connect, and engage in new ways.

 2026 Expo Lineup:

- April: Health & Wellness Expo

- May: Outdoor Adventures Expo

- June: Unity Through Diversity Expo

- July: Giving Back (Nonprofit Expo)

- August: Wedding & Events Expo

- September: Pet Expo

 April: Health & Wellness Expo

 Sponsored by Healthy Here, a Healthy Communities Collaborative

 Start the season with a focus on feeling your best, inside and out. The Health & Wellness Expo brings together a diverse mix of local businesses and practitioners dedicated to helping people improve their physical, mental, and emotional wellbeing.

 Visitors can explore everything from fitness programs and nutrition guidance to holistic health services, recovery tools, and self-care products. Expect interactive elements like mini demos, wellness tips, and opportunities to connect directly with experts who can support healthier lifestyles.

 Whether you're looking to build better habits, reduce stress, or discover new wellness resources, this expo offers something for everyone.

 Health & Wellness Vendor Application

 May: Outdoor Adventures Expo

 As the weather warms up, the Outdoor Adventures Expo taps into Chico's deep connection to the outdoors. This month celebrates exploration, recreation, and the spirit of adventure.

 Vendors will showcase outdoor gear, guided experiences, travel opportunities, and services that help people get outside and stay active. From hiking and biking to water sports and camping, visitors can discover new ways to experience the region and beyond.

 This expo also highlights environmental stewardship, connecting attendees with organizations and resources focused on protecting and preserving the natural spaces we all enjoy.

 Entertainment on the City Plaza Stage:

 May 7th -  Soul Posse Band

 May 14th & May 21st -  Dance Party by Breakbeat & Modelody DJ

 May 28th -  Emma & Will Band

 Outdoor Adventures Vendor Application

 June: Unity Through Diversity Expo

 The Unity Through Diversity Expo is a vibrant celebration of the cultures, traditions, and perspectives that shape our community.

 Throughout the month, visitors will experience a rich blend of cultural expression, through art, music, food, crafts, and storytelling. Local organizations, businesses, and artists will share their heritage and create opportunities for meaningful connection and learning.

 This expo is designed to bring people together, foster understanding, and highlight the beauty of Chico's diversity in an engaging and welcoming environment.

 Unity Through Diversity Vendor Application

 July: Giving Back Expo

 July is all about community impact. The Giving Back Expo shines a spotlight on the nonprofits, service groups, and mission-driven organizations working to make our region stronger, healthier, and more connected.

 Visitors will have the opportunity to meet the people behind these efforts, learn about important causes, and discover ways to get involved—whether through volunteering, donating, or simply spreading awareness.

 From youth programs and education to environmental efforts and social services, this expo highlights the many ways people are making a difference right here in Butte County.

 Giving Back Vendor Application

 August: Wedding & Events Expo

 Celebrate life's biggest moments with the Wedding & Events Expo, a curated showcase of the professionals who bring special occasions to life.

 This expo features local experts across every aspect of event planning, photographers, florists, planners, caterers, venues, entertainers, and more. Visitors can gather ideas, meet potential vendors, and get inspired for weddings, parties, corporate events, and celebrations of all kinds.

 With a focus on creativity and connection, this is the perfect opportunity to discover trusted local talent and start turning event visions into reality.

 Wedding & Events Vendor Application

 September: Pet Expo

 End the season on a high note with a celebration of our furry (and feathered) friends. The Pet Expo brings together pet-focused businesses and organizations dedicated to keeping animals healthy, happy, and thriving.

 Visitors can explore products, services, and resources including pet supplies, training, grooming, veterinary care, and adoption opportunities. It's also a chance to connect with local animal welfare groups and learn more about responsible pet ownership.

 Whether you're a devoted pet parent or simply an animal lover, this expo is a fun, engaging way to connect with Chico's pet-loving community.

 The Downtown Chico Business Association is proud to bring back one of Chico's most cherished summer traditions, the Friday Night Concert Series!  Bring Your Dinner, Stay for the Music!

Grab takeout from your favorite downtown restaurant and enjoy your meal picnic-style in the Plaza while the band plays. Support local restaurants and soak up the music-filled summer atmosphere.

 Whether you're dancing under the stars or relaxing with friends and family, there's no better place to be on a summer Friday night than Downtown Chico. Bring your appetite, your friends, your dancing shoes, and let's make it a summer to remember!

items.title: Community Tree Lighting Ceremony
items.content:
48th Annual Community Tree Lighting Ceremony

 Date: Friday, December 4, 2026

 Time:  6:00 - 8:00 PM (Ceremony from 6:00 - 6:30 PM)

 Location: Downtown Chico

  

 Overview

 Join us in the heart of Downtown Chico as we kick off the holiday season with one of our community's most cherished traditions—the annual Community Tree Lighting ! Since 1978, this magical evening has brought families, friends, and neighbors together to celebrate the spirit of the season beneath the glow of our community tree, located just outside the Chico City Plaza.

  

 2025 Event Itinerary

 This year, we are honored to welcome Chico Mayor Kasey Reynolds and our local media partners from The Blaze , who will help lead the festivities as we gather to celebrate joy, connection, and holiday cheer.

 Enjoy a festive lineup of local talent and seasonal fun, including:

- Live holiday music from the Sounds of the Valley Chorus

- Dazzling dance performances by Ballet Chico

- Hot cocoa, holiday beverages, treats, and goodies

- Shopping booths , gifts, and festive finds

- Letter-writing station for kids —we'll make sure every letter reaches Santa's workshop!

 And keep your eyes open… Santa himself may make a surprise appearance!

 And to make it even sweeter…

 To encourage shopping and dining downtown, the DCBA , in partnership with ChicoBag , is offering complimentary Candy Cane Shopping Bags to the first 50 shoppers who bring a receipt of $30 or more to the DCBA booth during the Community Tree Lighting Ceremony . Limit one per customer. Available while supplies last.

items.title: 5th Street Steakhouse
items.about:
Our Story

 5th Street Steakhouse is a locally owned and operated restaurant. We feature U.S.D.A. Prime Cuts of Beef, Fresh Seafood, a full service bar , and an extensive wine list. We pride ourselves in personal service matched with the finest quality food. We're looking forward to seeing you soon.

 The 5th Street Steakhouse is located in the picturesque downtown Chico. Since August 2000, the owners of this restaurant have prided themselves in providing the best quality cuisine in town. As a restaurant, our satisfaction comes from the finest service that will make any experience with us memorable.

 We are now excited to offer our services for private events during breakfast, lunch or dinner. The 5th Street Steakhouse Banquet Room is attached to the main dining room area, but provides a private section for your special event. Our facility is perfect for birthdays, wedding rehearsal dinners, anniversaries, religious celebrations, graduations, fundraisers, holiday parties, and private meetings. We also offer a full projection system for your convenience. With an array of menu options and an extensive wine list, we are sure that you will find us to be the ideal fit for your event.

items.title: Aca Taco
items.about:
Fresh and High-Quality Ingredients:

 At Aca Taco, we prioritize the use of fresh, locally sourced ingredients to ensure the highest quality in every bite. We believe in providing an exceptional dining experience that reflects our commitment to authenticity and flavor.

items.title: Ali Baba
items.about:
Welcome

 When I came to the U.S. many years ago, I missed the food of my country and culture,

 Syria, and a restaurant seemed like a natural way to satisfy my personal and financial needs at

 the same time. Some people recommended that to be successful in an American market, the food

 of Syria must be adapted to an American palate. The thought of the richness of the food that

 grew out of the cradle of civilization being changed was impossible to me, and more important,

 my family. The food the many generations of women in my family had faithfully passed down is

 the a priceless treasure and testament of the time tested recipes that are served to a new

 generation that finds the food incomparable to any other that can be found.

 To change the recipes is to deny the essence of the Syrian kitchen, for the food is more

 than a nutrition delivery system; it is food for the body and the soul.

 The Syrian dishes served at Ali Baba are not only a culinary experience, they are emotional. The spices unique to the meals  prepared at Ali Baba's have been carefully selected and imported from the Middle East to ensure the flavors are historically perfect. The love of family and culture is infused in the flavors in  every carefully rolled dolma (stuffed grape leaf), and the aromatic Basmati rice is prepared with  exactly the right spices to enhance rather than compete the flavors of the freshest ingredients.  The mothers that taught our cooks stressed the importance of history, culture, and family.  Each recipe serves as evidence of the love mothers have for their family, and we invite our  guests to join in the family of mankind as they partake of the food that grew out of the first civilazation of human history. e of the food that grew out of the first

 civilization of human history.

items.title: Amigos de Acapulco
items.about:
The Real Taste of Mexico

 Amigos de Aculpoco, authentic Mexican food you can count on with fresh home cooked flavor from the heart of Mexico. Family run and operated from the start, we hand make every dish from scratch with fresh ingredients.

items.title: Aonami Sustainable Sushi
items.about:
Aon­a­mi is Chico's first, and the nation's 10th, sus­tain­able sushi bar. They pledge to only use sus­tain­ably sourced seafood whether it is farmed or wild caught. Aon­a­mi fol­lows Mon­terey Bay Aquarium's lead and guid­ance as to what they can use in their sushi bar. It is their goal to con­tin­ue to learn and improve as a restau­rant for the future of our oceans.

 Their menu fea­tures raw and cooked options as well as a large veg­an selection.

items.title: B Street Public House
items.about:
About Us

 B Street Pub­lic House is a gas­trop­ub that serves New Amer­i­can fare in a rus­tic-chic loca­tion that fea­tures spa­cious patio seat­ing, a full bar, and a pop­u­lar week­end brunch.

 "B Street Pub­lic House is a dream cre­ation, a cof­fee bar that over the course of the day morphs from a spot for brunch and lunch to a din­ner house and even­tu­al­ly to a late-night music scene." ~Los Ange­les Times

items.title: Bella's Sports Pub
items.about:
Welcome to Bella's Sports Pub

 Purchased on New Year's Eve in 2008 and originally nestled at 134 Broadway St, Bella's Sports Pub has evolved into a staple of downtown Chico. Now proudly situated at 231 Main St since March 14, 2020, we've reimagined our classic dive into a vibrant hub for all things sports. Our inviting atmosphere, complete with multiple high-definition TVs and a spacious patio bar, ensures the ultimate game-day experience for every fan. Whether you're here to enjoy our mouth-watering wings, decadent tacos, or delicious smash burgers, you'll find the perfect pairing for cheering on your favorite team. And of course, no visit is complete without indulging in our iconic Soccer Mom Shot—a fan-favorite that's both refreshing and spirited, bringing regulars and newcomers together in a shared love for good times and great sports. So come on down to Bella's Sports Pub, where every game feels like a home game!

items.title: Big Hot Crab
items.about:
Have a hands-on meal.

 Big Hot Crab is a family-owned restaurant inspired by the Louisiana-style seafood boil, where heaps of delicious shellfish are cooked whole and served on the table for an indulgent, hands-on meal. Our mission has been to share this way of enjoying seafood, and since opening in 2015, we've been proudly serving seafood boils with our own twist on traditional Cajun flavors.

items.title: Boba TeaLicious
items.address: 225 Main St Ste M, Chico, California 95928-5481
items.about:
Located inside The Garden Walk in Downtown Chico.

 Boba Tealicious is the ultimate destination for boba lovers, offering an extensive selection of teas, smoothies, milk teas, fusion teas, and slushies. Known for their unique and delicious flavor combinations, like the crowd favorite Blueberry and Honeydew fusion with classic boba, it's no wonder customers rave about their wide range of options. Whether you're in the mood for a creamy Thai tea, a fragrant Taro+Lavender combo, or a refreshing fusion of two flavors, Boba Tealicious lets you customize your drink to perfection.

 Customers also love the quality of the boba itself—cooked just right for that perfect chewy texture—and the balanced sweetness of their beverages. The welcoming, cozy atmosphere and excellent customer service add to the experience, making it a go-to spot in Chico. While tucked away in a small mall, those in the know appreciate the clean, inviting vibe and are always coming back for more. If you're looking for a boba experience that's as delicious as it is memorable, Boba Tealicious is the place to be!

items.title: Bodega Slice Shop
items.about:
Expe­ri­ence authen­tic New York-style piz­za in the heart of Down­town Chico at Bode­ga Slice Shop.

 Known for their per­fect­ly craft­ed slices and whole pies, Bode­ga offers dai­ly favorites along­side cre­ative rotat­ing spe­cials. The casu­al, counter-ser­vice atmos­phere cap­tures the essence of a clas­sic NYC pizze­ria, mak­ing it a per­fect stop for a quick lunch or fam­i­ly dinner.

items.title: Broadway Heights California Cuisine
items.about:
About Broadway Heights California Cuisine

 Broadway Heights is a beloved dining icon in the heart of beautiful downtown Chico!

 A tasty option for students or downtown business lunches alike, Broadway Heights has created a legacy of consistently delicious sandwiches, soups, salads and more, focusing on house-made dishes, all from scratch. And, we're introducing new entrees and dinner items. Our baked focaccia sandwiches are Chico-famous!

 Our constant goal is to continue this tradition, and refine the downtown dining experience. We think quality dining in Chico should be comfortable, welcoming and inviting without sacrificing quality ingredients or flavor.

 We also love to cater! The restaurant, located in the beautiful and historic Phoenix Building in downtown Chico, is available for private parties up to 100 people (room rental fees may apply), and includes all linens, dishware, flatware, glasses, buffet or full table service.

 Off-site catering is also available. We can come to you with a range of services, from simply dropping off food for a meeting to providing a fully staffed banquet.

 All food is prepared with the highest quality ingredients to create a fresh and healthy blend that is sure to delight even the most discriminating palate. Broadway Heights is consistently voted "Best in Chico."

items.title: Burgers & Brew
items.about:
Enamored by the growing craft-brew industry and frustrated with the average draft beer selection, Derar Zawaydeh and Philippe Masoud wanted a place to enjoy craft beers on tap and close to home. After creating several successful Crepeville restaurants in the Sacramento Area, they applied their expertise to untapped passion.

 Founded in 2007, it wasn't long before Burgers and Brew started winning awards for its beer selection and burgers. Derar and Philippe attribute the acclaim to their meticulous approach to the dining experience-- no ingredient is too small to tailor for each dish. Their burgers are made with 100% Niman Ranch meats and the freshest ingredients, their sauces and dressings are made in-house, and their dining spaces are designed to best serve each location. Comprehensive doesn't begin to describe the Burgers and Brew experience.

 Another key to success for Burgers and Brew's aesthetic has been dedication to the brew. Derar and Philippe are determined to bring world-class beers like Russian River Brewing Co.'s Pliny the Elder and Ballast Point's Sculpin IPA to the tap as well as local favorites like Sierra Nevada, Berryessa Brewing, and Sudwerks.

 But, with an unbridled passion for creating sensational dining experiences from scratch, they had to make their own beer as well. Derar and Philippe opened Olde Ritual Brewing Company in 2011 with their inaugural brew, Rapture- an Imperial Brown Ale, only available at Burgers and Brew.

items.title: Café Coda
items.about:
Our History

 Established in 2006.

 Cafe Coda was conceptualized and launched by Eric Ryan Danielli and a small army of dedicated friends and family. Since it's inception, Cafe Coda has fulfilled a niche as a premier location for both great food and great live entertainment in Chico, Ca.

items.title: Celestino's New York Pizza
items.about:
Our History

 Celestino's Pizza opened for Business on April Fool's Day, April 1, 1997.

 They had a simple plan: Offer Authentic New York thin-crust Pizza and they will come!

 The Chico-Downtown store is still owned & operated by the original owners!

items.title: Chada Thai
items.address: 117 W 2nd St Ste B, Chico, California 95928-5303
items.about: We have been established since 1999 and we are family owned and run. We are authentic and only use fresh produce and ingredients to make our food that is always made to order. We also provide a full vegetarian menu besides having meat and seafood dishes.

items.title: Crush
items.about:
About Us

 Crush Dining Room and Lounge invites you to indulge in an unforgettable dining experience like no other. Fusing traditional Italian flavors with fresh, locally-sourced, seasonal ingredients, our vibrant menu is a celebration of bold tastes and culinary creativity.

 Step into a chic, contemporary atmosphere where soft lighting and a spacious lounge invite you to relax and immerse yourself in the energy of the restaurant. Whether you're savoring our expertly crafted dishes or sipping on a glass from our extensive wine list—featuring over 100 selections—each moment is designed to delight the most discerning palates.

 Join us for a dining experience that is as unforgettable as it is unique. Reservations are highly recommended to ensure your place in this extraordinary setting. Treat yourself to the best—your senses will thank you.

items.title: Franky's Chico
items.about:
Welcome to Franky's!

 Franky's was built with "family" in mind! Nick Andrew and Kevin Riley built Franky's back in December 1992 with the concept of a casual and friendly "Cheers" type atmosphere in a restaurant setting. When Nick was young and still living at home his mother would make spaghetti every Friday night. Today Franky's still uses the family recipes for their delicious meat sauces.

 Franky's offers a diverse wine selection and a full bar that is unrivaled in the North State. With so many different flavors available Franky's is sure to meet the needs of any discriminating palette. Franky's bartenders are renowned for their knowledge of cocktail preparation and offer a dizzying variety of martinis for those that like their drinks, "Up," "Over," "Neat" or whatever your preference may be.

 The large menu at Franky's offers everything from made-fresh daily pasta, the truly famous Caesar Salad to fresh-tossed pizzas made with quality and pride to be the best around. With deals for students (Late Night pizza has been known to save lives) and family's too (the Family Special feeds four or more people with four salads, a platter of spaghetti and a large 18" 3-item pizza) there is always a meal to satisfy everyone at Franky's.

 Franky's, the perfect choice for your next date, business lunch, family dinner, birthday, or whatever your occasion may be. Get together and enjoy your friends, family and fine food and drink at Franky's.

items.title: Gogi's Café
hours: Saturday 11?AM–8?PM Sunday Closed Monday 11?AM–8?PM Tuesday 11?AM–8?PM Wednesday 11?AM–8?PM Thursday 11?AM–8?PM Friday 11?AM–8?PM
items.about: Gogi's Cafe is a family-run North Indian Restaurant located in the heart of Downtown Chico in Northern California's Central Valley.  It is created and run by a husband & wife team of certified chefs from New Delhi, India who prepare and serve gourmet menu options from scratch every morning, or on order, with authentic herbs and spices.

items.title: Grana
hours: Saturday 11:30?AM–8:30?PM Sunday Closed Monday Closed Tuesday Closed Wednesday 11:30?AM–8:30?PM Thursday 11:30?AM–8:30?PM Friday 11:30?AM–8:30?PM Suggest new hours
items.about:
Our dishes are cooked in our wood fired oven. The wood fire oven keeps our Neapolitan pizza faithful to Italian roots. With a softer center and a crisp outer crust, these pizzas are quite simple. Fresh dough, made daily with Italian 00 flour, high-quality tomatoes, Di Stefano fresh mozzarella, olive oil, basil, and sea salt create our Margerita pizza. Or try one of our more modern pizza creations.

 Grana offers a seasonal menu, featuring organic and sustainable products from our local farmers. We encourage you to support your local Chico Certified Farmers Market and Buy Fresh, Buy Local.

items.title: Juice Unwind Caribbean Barbeque
items.about: Juice Unwind Caribbean Barbecue in Downtown Chico is a standout destination for bold, flavorful Caribbean cuisine and warm, welcoming service. Guests can't get enough of the tender, juicy meats like jerk chicken, brisket, oxtail, and ribs, each dish packed with authentic island flavor and cooked to perfection. Portion sizes are generous, often enough to share or take home for later, making it a great value. The sides, including mac and cheese, rice and beans, collard greens, and Jamaican patties, perfectly complement the main dishes and round out a satisfying meal. The staff is consistently praised for their friendliness and efficiency, and the vibrant, casual setting with indoor and outdoor seating makes for a relaxed, enjoyable dining experience. Whether you're a fan of classic barbecue or looking to explore Caribbean flavors, Juice Unwind delivers a memorable and delicious experience every time.

Since that day we have been doing just that and haven't looked back.

items.title: Main Street Pizza
hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 11:45 AM - 8 PM, 10 PM - 12 AM
items.about:
A boutique pizzeria and hidden gem in Downtown Chico

 Welcome to Main Street Pizza, a hidden gem in downtown Chico, California. Our boutique pizzeria is a haven for pizza enthusiasts. We pride ourselves on crafting mouthwatering pizzas that transport your taste buds to a world of gastronomic delight. Our skilled pizzaiolos have perfected the art of pizza-making, blending traditional techniques with innovative flavors for unforgettable culinary creations. Pizza is more than just food; it's a shared experience that brings people together. Join our vibrant community of pizza lovers, where every slice is savored and cherished. Discover why Main Street Pizzeria is beloved by locals and visitors alike.

 About the Owner

 The owner responded by saying:

 "Hi, I'm Chris Durkin, owner of Main Street Pizza.

 I took over Main Street Pizza in November 2025 with one clear goal:

 protect what people already loved, and rebuild the rest the right way.

 This shop has been part of downtown Chico for a long time. Late nights, big slices, familiar faces. I didn't come in to reinvent it overnight, I came in to stabilize it, upgrade the systems behind the scenes, and slowly raise the bar on quality, consistency, and service.

 I've spent over 20 years working in restaurants, from the floor to the kitchen to management. I'm hands-on by nature, you'll see me making dough, running the oven, fixing equipment, and talking with customers. If something's broken, I'd rather learn how to fix it than ignore it.

 Since taking over, we've:

- Modernized and introduced online ordering

- Added Detroit-style pizzas and lunch options

- Extended hours and launched delivery

- Focused on better ingredients and cleaner execution

 Main Street Pizza is still the place for a late-night slice, but it's also becoming a place you can count on any day of the week.

 Thanks for supporting a locally owned, family-run business.

 I hope to see you in the shop.

 - Chris"

 What do you love about being in Downtown Chico?

 "Owning a store downtown, right in the heart of the city, is something a lot of people dream about. I love being close to the energy, events, foot traffic, and the people who make Chico what it is. Being downtown means being part of the community, not just operating a business, and that's something I don't take lightly."

 What is your favorite Downtown Chico event?

 "Farmers Market"

items.title: Sicilian Café
items.about:
Fresh Italian cuisine served with old world hospitality

 Established in 1984 in a small white house on Walnut Street, Sicilian Café has been a North State favorite for 41 years. We feature recipes passed down through generations, utilizing fresh local produce, delicious pastas, chicken, beef, and seafood dishes served with fine wines and craft cocktails. Happy Hour and à la carte dishes are available in our bar, or you can dine with a three-course dinner in our elegant dining room. You'll enjoy fresh Italian cuisine served with old world hospitality.

 About the Owners

 James Taylor and his mother Cathy opened Sicilian Café back in 1984. Forty-one years later, James and his wife Misti, along with head chef Sam Watroba, are still hands-on owners who are at the restaurant every day.

 "Our favorite part of what we do is the amazing people we get to see every day. We have customers that have been dining with us since we first opened in 1984 and now bring their children and grandchildren in for family celebrations. We love that we have new guests every day too. We consider our guests to be friends and family, and it makes what we do special."

 What do you love about being in Downtown Chico?

 "We love Downtown Chico and the wonderful sense of community it brings. The mix of young college students, young families, and new and old residents of Chico bring vitality to downtown. Chico has grown so much over the years, but when you come downtown you always see someone you know, old friends and new friends. That is what makes Downtown so special."

 What is your favorite Downtown Chico event?

 "Christmas Preview"

items.about:
Our mission is to create a warm, inviting, and cozy dining experience right in the heart of Downtown Chico. We take pride in serving unique, comforting classics made in-house with the freshest, highest-quality ingredients. Whether you're stopping by for a drink and a meal, enjoying a family dinner, or celebrating a private event, we're delighted to be part of your special moments.

 As a locally owned and operated family business, proudly part of the Hotel Diamond family and conveniently located on the first floor, we invite you to slow down, relax, and truly enjoy the company of those around you. items.title: Woodstock's Pizza
hours: Happy Hour: Mon-Fri 4-6 pm 1/2 off all pitchers
items.about:
The Ultimate Pizza Experience since 1983

 Our mission is to earn loyal customers and team members who uniquely regard Woodstock's as the Ultimate Pizza Experience.

 How we earn it

- Prepare every customer's meal with individual attention.

- Use the freshest, best tasting ingredients.

- Consistently create outstanding, unique pizzas and accompaniments that are widely-regarded as the best ever, anywhere!

- Treat each and every customer with friendliness, respect and courtesy.

- Provide outstanding value that keeps customers returning with uncommon frequency.

- Be recognized as a fun and outstanding place to work.

- Provide a fun-filled festive atmosphere.

 Woodstock's will encourage our team members to work hard and have fun!

 We'll strengthen the communities we serve by giving back and fostering partnerships with local non-profit organizations.

 And we'll always make sure you get the tastiest pizza around, any way you like it.

 California grown, fresh from the vine tomatoes are enhanced with a secret blend of spices to give Woodstock's sauce a unique, zesty taste.

 About the Owner

 It was 1977… when Chuck Woodstock opened the first Woodstock's Pizza near Oregon State in Corvallis, Oregon. His goal was to create a fun, student-oriented pizzeria that dished up amazing pies using a unique, zesty marinara recipe. From the beginning he focused on fresh, hand-tossed dough with a unique folded-over crust with sauce inside. He insisted on putting more toppings on the pizza than any of his competitors, so you get a taste of every topping in each bite.

 He quickly gained a loyal following, and partnered with Mike Chew in 1980 to build their next restaurant in San Luis Obispo, and then came Woodstock's Isla Vista.

 Meanwhile back in Corvallis, Jeff Ambrose was a recent college grad without a full-time job. Delivering pizzas seemed like a good way to keep from starving. But it turned out to be much more than he expected —

 In 1982, Chuck and Chew traveled to Chico and took a look around the town and knew they had found exactly what they wanted. In fact, they were so excited about the location that Chuck bought the building right across the street from Chico State and named it Woodstock Plaza.

 Woodstock's Pizza opened in August of 1983 and it quickly became an institution in Chico.

 Chuck and Chew then partnered with Jeff to open a store near SDSU. Meanwhile, they worked on a new restaurant in Davis.

 Sadly, Chuck and Chew passed away in a private plane crash in 1985, leaving the company in the hands of their family members. Eventually the families promoted Jeff to President of the company. In 2001, Jeff and his wife, Laura, purchased all five California restaurants. (The original Corvallis restaurant is solely owned by Chuck's wife, Carol Lee Woodstock.)

 Since that time, we have added two new restaurants, in Santa Cruz and in Pacific Beach, San Diego.

 Woodstock's has an employee stock ownership plan, and encourages all managers to become owners. The company is currently ranked #1 among independent pizzerias nationwide. True to Chuck's legacy, we are still committed to offering the highest quality ingredients, making our dough fresh daily in-house, keeping our signature sauce a secret, and working hard to create loyal customers and team members, while having fun in the process.

 What do you love about being in Downtown Chico?

 Taylor Davis, Manager, responded by saying:

 "Vibrant mix of local businesses, unique restaurants, and cultural attractions like live music venues and art galleries. The area is also known for its strong sense of community, historic architecture, public art, and a thriving farmer's market, all of which create a unique, welcoming atmosphere with a slower pace than a big city."

 What is your favorite Downtown Chico event?

 "Taste of Downtown"
